leander-isd-schools-review-2026

Published on 2/28/2026

Leander Independent School District: A Comprehensive Review of its 47 Schools

Leander Independent School District (LISD) is a prominent educational institution serving a rapidly growing community in Central Texas. With a commitment to academic excellence and student success, LISD operates a diverse network of schools across Leander, Cedar Park, and surrounding areas. This blog post provides an in-depth look at the district's educational landscape, including a breakdown of its schools, their distribution, and insights into their performance.

LISD at a Glance: 47 Schools Strong

According to official data, Leander ISD currently operates 47 schools, catering to a wide range of student needs from pre-kindergarten through 12th grade. The district's structure includes elementary, middle, high, and alternative schools, each playing a crucial role in fostering a supportive learning environment.

School Distribution by Level

The distribution of schools across different levels within LISD is as follows:

  • Elementary Schools: 28
  • Middle Schools: 9
  • High Schools: 6
  • Alternative Schools: 4

This distribution highlights a strong foundation at the elementary level, providing ample access to early education, while also offering comprehensive options for middle and high school students. The presence of alternative schools underscores the district's dedication to providing diverse educational pathways tailored to individual student requirements.

Top-Rated Schools in LISD

Leander ISD boasts several highly-rated schools, recognized for their academic programs, student support, and overall educational quality. Based on GreatSchools ratings, here are some of the top performers in each category:

| Level | Top Rated Schools (GreatSchools Rating) | | :------------- | :-------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Elementary | Deer Creek (10/10), Laura Welch Bush (10/10), River Ridge (9/10) | | Middle | Stiles (10/10), Canyon Ridge (9/10), Henry (8/10) | | High | Vandegrift (9/10), Rouse (8/10), Vista Ridge (8/10) | | Alternative | Early College High School (7/10) |

These ratings reflect the dedication of educators, staff, and the community in providing exceptional learning experiences for LISD students.

Leander ISD School List (2026)

Here is a comprehensive list of all 47 schools within the Leander Independent School District, categorized by level, along with their addresses:

Elementary Schools (28)

  1. Akin Elementary School: 3261 Barley Road, Leander, 78641
  2. Bagdad Elementary School: 800 Deercreek Ln., Leander, 78641
  3. Block House Creek Elementary School: 401 Creek Run, Leander, 78641
  4. Camacho Elementary School: 501 Municipal Dr., Leander, 78641
  5. Cox Elementary School: 1001 Brushy Creek Rd., Cedar Park, 78613
  6. Cypress Elementary School: 2900 El Salido Pkwy., Cedar Park, 78613
  7. Deer Creek Elementary School: 2420 Zeppelin Dr., Cedar Park, 78613
  8. Faubion Elementary School: 1209 Cypress Creek Rd., Cedar Park, 78613
  9. Giddens Elementary School: 1500 Timberwood Dr., Cedar Park, 78613
  10. Grandview Hills Elementary School: 12024 Vista Parke Dr., Austin, 78726
  11. Hisle Elementary School: 1913 Frontenac St, Leander, TX 78641
  12. Knowles Elementary School: 2101 Cougar Country Dr., Cedar Park, 78613
  13. Larkspur Elementary School: 424 Rusk Bluff Avenue, Leander, 78641
  14. Laura Welch Bush Elementary School: 12600 Country Trails Ln., Austin, 78732
  15. Mason Elementary School: 1501 N. Lakeline Blvd., Cedar Park, 78613
  16. Naumann Elementary School: 1201 Brighton Bend, Cedar Park, 78613
  17. North Elementary School: 1840 Pleasant Hill Rd., Leander, 78641
  18. Parkside Elementary School: 301 Garner Park Dr., Georgetown, 78628
  19. Plain Elementary School: 501 South Brook Dr., Leander, 78641
  20. Pleasant Hill Elementary School: 1800 Horizon Park Blvd., Leander, 78641
  21. Reagan Elementary School: 1700 E. Park Street, Cedar Park, 78613
  22. Reed Elementary School: 1515 Little Elm Trail, Cedar Park, 78613
  23. River Place Elementary School: 6500 Sitio Del Rio Blvd., Austin, 78730
  24. River Ridge Elementary School: 12900 Tierra Grande Trail, Austin, 78732
  25. Rutledge Elementary School: 11501 Staked Plains Dr., Austin, 78717
  26. Steiner Ranch Elementary School: 4001 N. Quinlan Park Rd., Austin, 78732
  27. Tarvin Elementary School: 1280 Logan Del Way, Leander, 78641
  28. Westside Elementary School: 300 Ryan Jordan Ln., Cedar Park, 78613

Middle Schools (9)

  1. Canyon Ridge Middle School: 12601 Country Trails, Austin, 78732
  2. Cedar Park Middle School: 2100 Sun Chase Blvd., Cedar Park, 78613
  3. Danielson Middle School: 1061 Collaborative Way, Leander, 78641
  4. Four Points Middle School: 9700 McNeil Dr., Austin, 78750
  5. Henry Middle School: 100 N. Vista Ridge Blvd., Cedar Park, 78613
  6. Leander Middle School: 410 S. West Dr., Leander, 78641
  7. Running Brushy Middle School: 2303 Lakeline Blvd., Cedar Park, 78613
  8. Stiles Middle School: 3250 Barley Rd., Leander, 78641
  9. Wiley Middle School: 1526 Raider Way, Leander, 78641

High Schools (6)

  1. Cedar Park High School: 2150 Cypress Creek Rd., Cedar Park, 78613
  2. Glenn High School: 1320 Collaborative Way, Leander, 78641
  3. Leander High School: 3301 S. Bagdad Rd., Leander, 78641
  4. Rouse High School: 1222 Raider Way, Leander, 78641
  5. Vandegrift High School: 9500 McNeil Dr., Austin, 78750
  6. Vista Ridge High School: 200 S. Vista Ridge Blvd., Cedar Park, 78613

Alternative Schools (4)

  1. Geiger Compass Center: 1775 W. New Hope Dr., Cedar Park, 78613
  2. Early College High School: 696 Main St. Bldg. 10, Leander, 78641
  3. LEO Center: 300 S. West Dr., Leander, 78641
  4. New Hope High School: 401 S. West Dr., Leander, 78641

Conclusion

Leander ISD continues to be a cornerstone of education in its community, offering a comprehensive and diverse range of schools to meet the needs of its growing student population. The district's commitment to academic excellence, coupled with its focus on student support and innovative programs, makes it a desirable choice for families seeking quality education in Central Texas.
